name: Reverse Wave
preceding_figure_names: Basic Weave, Bounce Fallaway with Weave Ending, Closed Impetus and Feather Finish, Fallaway Reverse and Slip Pivot, Feather Step, Hover Cross, Hover Feather, Natural Hover Telemark, Natural Telemark, Natural Twist Turn, Natural Weave, Natural Zig-Zag from PP, Open Telemark and Feather Ending, Open Telemark, Natural Turn, Outside Swivel and Feather Ending, Reverse Turn, Top Spin, Weave from PP
following_figure_names: Feather Step, Basic Weave (after step 4), Closed Impetus and Feather Finish (after step 6), Open Impetus (after step 6), Hover Feather (after step 8), Curved Feather to Back Feather (at corner)
notes: There are five alignments of the Reverse Wave: Facing LOD 3/8 between 1-3, 1/8 between 4-6, 3/8 between 7-8 (or less at corner); Facing LOD 3/8 between 1-3, 1/4 between 4-6 to back DW of new LOD, 1/4 between 7-8; Facing DW 1/2 between 1-3, then continue as in alignment (1); Facing DW 1/2 between 1-3, then continue as in alignment (2); Facing DC 3/8 between 1-3, 1/4 between 4-6 to back the new LOD, 3/8 between 7-8. Alignments 2,4, and 5 are used to turn a corner. Extended Reverse Wave can be added with SQQ steps: Man continues BLOD with TH, LF back; Q: T, RF back, R shoulder leading; Q: TH, LF back in CBMP; Lady continues facing LOD, poise not back weight, shoulder dancing in the back of the feet; then continue from step 4 again. Head can keep head left or shape to R, feeling legs dancing ahead of body.

steps.action: LF fwd | Facing LOD | HT | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise e/o 1
RF to side | Backing Wall | T
LF back | Curving towards LOD | T
RF to side small step (Heel Pull) | Facing DC | H, IE of foot, whole foot then IE of LF
LF fwd | Facing DC | H | Body turn to L | CBM | --
RF fwd | Facing LOD | HT | start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | --
LF to side | Backing DC | TH, then IE of T of RF
RF back having brushed to LF | Backing DC | T | Body turn to L | Sway(S) | CBM | --
